label: 2026-06-05 close
title: Daily FI Market Tape | 2026-06-05 收盤
summary: 非農 +172k 明顯高於約 +80k 共識，2Y 升至 4.17%、5Y 升至 4.29%，Fed 保留升息選項的定價重新壓回前端與中段。
10Y 收 4.55%、30Y 收 5.01%，2s10s 與 5s30s 同步壓平，顯示長端買盤仍受期限溢價與供給壓力約束。
SOX -10.3%、NDX -4.8%、LQD -0.6%，跨資產壓力主要是折現率與資金成本上修，不是乾淨的避險買債。
CBC USD/TWD 收 31.475，台幣計價海外債 ETF 有匯率換算支撐，但利率上行與信用代理指標下跌會抵銷部分 NAV 助益。

headline.primary: 強非農推升前端利率，熊平壓住長天期 ETF
headline.secondary: 2Y 4.17%、10Y 4.55%、2s10s 38bp；中段先觀察，20Y+ 等 CPI、30Y 與油價確認。

positioning.label: Duration / 5Y-10Y UST
positioning.best_expression: 10Y 4.55%-4.60% 是 watch zone；小部位觀察，不等於全面加碼。

positioning.label: Curve / 20Y+ UST
positioning.best_expression: 30Y 未跌破 4.95 前不追長端；5% 上方仍是風險補償。
positioning.risk_trigger: 只有 30Y < 4.95 且 Brent < 92 才提高長端；30Y > 5.05 時降風險。

positioning.label: Credit / IG
positioning.best_expression: LQD > 108 時保留 IG 收益與品質，不用 HY 景氣曝險承擔風險。
positioning.risk_trigger: LQD < 108 代表 IG 總報酬壓力擴大，信用曝險需降速。

positioning.label: Credit / HY 景氣曝險
positioning.best_expression: HYG > 79 且 SOX 企穩前，不用高票息追景氣曝險。
positioning.risk_trigger: HYG < 79 或 LQD/HYG 同跌時，優先回到高品質與流動性。

positioning.label: FX / NAV
positioning.best_expression: USD/TWD 31.40 / 31.55 只做台幣 NAV 換算歸因，不作單獨加碼理由。
positioning.risk_trigger: FX 利多必須同時檢查 UST 價格與 LQD；台幣走弱不等於總報酬利多。

driver_decomposition.signal: 非農 +172k 對約 +80k 共識；失業率 4.3%。
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: 這是 06/05 的第一階 driver：市場重新定價 Fed 保留升息選項，2Y-5Y 最直接承壓。

driver_decomposition.signal: CPI 尚未公布；swap 隱含約 4.3% y/y。
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: CPI 是前瞻觸發，不是已發生的主因；低於 4.1% 才能把 5Y-10Y 從觀察推向加碼。
長端買盤未取得主導，20Y+ ETF 的價格彈性仍受期限溢價、供給與避險需求不足限制。

driver_decomposition.signal: SOX -10.3%、Nasdaq 100 -4.8%，跌幅大於 S&P 500 與 Dow。
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: 跨資產訊號指向折現率上修壓縮長久期現金流，而不是傳統 risk-off 形成長端買盤。

driver_decomposition.signal: Anthropic 晶片融資與 SpaceX IPO 需求顯示大型資本吸收仍在。
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: AI 融資不是 06/05 UST 第一階主因；它是檢查長端資本需求、IG 替代供給與期限溢價黏性的結構性背景。

driver_decomposition.signal: Brent 周五 93.09，亞洲盤反彈至約 95.60。
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: 油價是長端修復與 Fed 語氣的外生約束；92 下方降溫，98 上方重新提高長端風險補償。
美元偏強支撐台幣 NAV 換算，但正式判讀仍需拆 FX translation、UST price return 與 credit spread return。

sections.title: 一、美債收盤與曲線變化
sections.takeaway: 強非農推高前端與中段，長端買盤仍受期限溢價約束。
paragraphs: 6 月 5 日美債收盤的主線是勞動市場強於預期後，Fed 政策路徑重新往較高利率區間定價。官方 CMT 顯示 2Y 收 4.17%、日升 12bp，5Y 收 4.29%、日升 11bp，10Y 收 4.55%、日升 8bp，20Y 與 30Y 分別在 5.03% 與 5.01%。升幅由前端與中段領先，代表市場先處理政策利率路徑，再把長端風險補償重新調高。 這對中短天期 ETF 的意義是進場殖利率改善，但利率波動也會更快反映在價格；若沒有通膨降溫配合，carry 的吸引力不足以單獨抵銷前端再上行風險。

曲線訊號同樣偏向熊平。2s10s 收 38bp、日內壓平 4bp，5s30s 收 72bp、日內壓平 7bp，10s30s 收 46bp、日內壓平 4bp。這種型態表示 2Y-5Y 對 Fed 定價反應最直接，30Y 則仍受期限溢價、供給吸收與油價通膨溢價約束。對 ETF 來說，中段 Duration 的波動來源較清楚，20Y+ 的價格彈性仍較不乾淨。 這也使不同天期 ETF 的歸因差異變大：5Y-10Y 主要看 Fed 與 CPI，20Y+ 則同時承擔供給、期限溢價與長端買盤不足。

從一周與一個月變化看，這不是單日噪音。2Y 周升 19bp、月升 24bp，5Y 周升 16bp、月升 21bp，10Y 周升 10bp、月升 12bp；曲線 2s10s 月內壓平 12bp，5s30s 月內壓平 18bp。累積變化顯示市場已從等待降息時點，轉向檢查 Fed 是否需要保留升息選項。若 CPI 再確認通膨黏性，前端與中段仍有再上修空間。 對配置節奏而言，周/月變化比單日收盤更重要，因為它顯示市場已經連續調整政策路徑假設；短線反彈若沒有資料確認，容易只是部位修正。

長端沒有在股市大跌時出現乾淨的避險買盤，是本日最重要的交叉訊號。30Y 收在 5.01%，20Y 收在 5.03%，即使 Nasdaq 100 與 SOX 大跌，長端殖利率仍維持高位。這代表長天期美債 ETF 不能只靠股市壓力期待 NAV 修復；價格回升需要同時看到 CPI 不再上衝、油價回落、供給壓力緩和，以及 30Y 重新跌回 5% 下方。 因此長端 ETF 的勝率不只取決於股市風險情緒，也取決於長端投資人是否願意接受低於 5% 的 30Y；這個條件目前還沒有被確認。

資料本身提供了前端重新定價的直接理由。非農 +172k 明顯高於約 +80k 共識，失業率維持 4.3%，4 月就業還上修到 +179k；平均時薪年增 3.4% 雖然不是失控，但不足以抵銷就業驚喜。這組組合使市場很難沿用「勞動市場快速轉弱」的降息敘事，2Y 與 5Y 因此承受最大壓力。 對短中天期 ETF，這意味著勞動市場仍是第一階敏感因子；若初領失業金或薪資沒有同步轉弱，前端利率回落的持續性會受到限制。這一段只支持 watch zone，還不足以把 5Y-10Y 升級成全面 add。

油價是長端修復的第二個約束。Brent 周五收 93.09、日跌 2.0%，WTI 收 90.54、日跌 2.7%，表面上有助通膨溢價降溫；但亞洲盤 Brent 反彈到約 95.60，表示地緣與能源風險仍未完全退出 Fed 反應函數。若 Brent 回到 98 上方，損益平衡通膨與實質所得壓力會重新升高，長端 Duration 部位需要更嚴格的停損與分批節奏。 這條線會直接影響 10Y 與 30Y 的風險補償，也會影響高配息債券 ETF 的本金波動，因為能源壓力會先透過殖利率而非配息率反映。

因此，06/05 close 對台灣債券型 ETF 的第一層訊息是中短天期利率風險升高，長天期仍受期限溢價約束，信用與成長股長久期資產同步承壓。10Y 4.55% 已提供較高 carry，但它仍需 CPI 與 FOMC 語句確認；30Y 5.01% 則提醒 20Y+ ETF 的短期價格風險仍大於單純殖利率吸引力。 因此當天較合理的部位語氣是保留觀察與分批，而不是把高殖利率直接視為全面加碼訊號；確認點仍在 CPI、FOMC、30Y 與 Brent。

sections.title: 二、美債走勢解讀
sections.takeaway: CPI 與 FOMC 語句決定 10Y 4.55% 是進場區還是中繼壓力。
paragraphs: 利率定價的核心變化，是 Fed 反應函數從等待降息條件，轉為重新證明抗通膨可信度。就業公布後，市場已把年底前 25bp 升息納入定價，10 月升息機率也被推到約六成。這不代表 Fed 一定升息，而是代表前端不能再把降息當成單向路徑；持有 5Y-10Y 或更長天期 ETF 時，收益率提高與價格波動必須一起評估。 這種環境下，ETF 投資人要先決定承擔哪一段曲線風險，而不是只比較票面殖利率；前端路徑不穩時，長天期商品的 NAV 會被放大波動。

CPI 是下一個分水嶺。swap 隱含本周 CPI 約 4.3% y/y，若公布值接近這個水準，10Y 4.55% 可能只是中繼壓力，市場會重新測試 4.65%，並要求 6 月 16-17 日 FOMC 聲明移除偏降息語氣。若 CPI 低於 4.1%，非農衝擊缺乏通膨確認，前端與中段才有較高機率修復。 這也是 5Y-10Y 比 20Y+ 更適合作為第一觀察區的原因：中段可以較快反映政策預期修復，長端則還要等待期限溢價與供給訊號轉弱。

政策溝通的重要性同步上升。當 Goldman 已移除今年降息預期，而 Citi 仍維持較偏降息的路徑，機構預測分歧本身就會放大每一句官方措辭的市場敏感度。2Y 是否站穩 4.15%、5Y 是否守住 4.30%，會比單日股市跌幅更能反映政策尾端風險；就業與 Fed 定價應被視為同一條政策機制。 若這些價位繼續守高，代表市場仍要求政策不確定性的補償；若它們回落，才代表投資人重新接受降息路徑與中段 Duration。

曲線交易上，追空前端與追買長端都不乾淨。2s10s 已壓到 38bp，5s30s 已壓到 72bp，壓平交易已走出一段；但 30Y 還在 5.01%，若供給與期限溢價繼續黏著，過早買入 20Y+ 等於承擔另一種風險。較合理的觀察區是 5Y-10Y，長端只有在 30Y 重新跌破 4.95% 且油價回落時才提高信心。 對 ETF 實作而言，曲線斜率不是獨立交易標籤，而是決定中段與長端哪一段更適合承擔風險；當 30Y 未確認時，長端比例不宜擴太快。

長端壓力不是單一事件，而是多個風險補償同時存在。財政供給、長天期發行吸收能力、MBS 避險調整與油價通膨溢價，都會影響 30Y 是否能穩定回到 5% 下方。AI 融資與大型 IPO 供給不應被視為 06/05 UST 賣壓的同級原因；它們比較適合放在 structural capital absorption check，用來檢查長端資本需求與 IG 替代供給是否讓 30Y 更難回到 5% 以下。台灣投資人若持有 20Y+ ETF，這段風險會透過價格波動直接反映在台幣 NAV；若同時持有高配息產品，更要拆開 income 與本金損益。

加拿大就業 +87.8k、失業率降至 6.6% 是輔助證據。它不是 UST 的核心 driver，但說明北美需求韌性不是美國單點故事，全球前端利率都在重新檢查需求尚未冷卻的風險。對台灣投資人而言，這會影響美元債 ETF 的折現率，也會影響央行政策敘事能否快速回到降息主軸。 這種輔助證據可以提高對前端利率黏著的信心，但仍不應凌駕美國 CPI 與 FOMC；配置上只把它當作降低過早加碼勝率的背景條件。

美元走強與黃金下跌也支持實質利率重新上行的解讀。DXY 升 0.66%，黃金跌 3.1%，代表市場重估美元利差與實質收益。對台幣計價海外債 ETF，美元偏強有利台幣 NAV 換算，但實質利率上行會壓住 UST 與 IG bond 的美元價格；兩條損益來源必須分開看，不能互相抵銷後直接得出正面結論。 因此 FX 對 NAV 的正貢獻只能放在歸因的一條線，不能覆蓋利率和信用價格的負貢獻；這點對未避險或低避險比率產品尤其重要。

sections.title: 三、其他市場動向
sections.takeaway: 跨資產壓力指向折現率與資金成本上修，亞洲/EM 先作傳導觀察。
paragraphs: 風險資產的訊號集中在長久期現金流被較高折現率壓縮。Nasdaq 100 跌 4.8%、SOX 跌 10.3%，跌幅遠大於 Dow 的 1.3%，顯示市場先賣對利率最敏感的 AI 與半導體現金流。這對 FI 的含義是成長股與長天期債券同時面對較高折現率，而不是形成天然對沖。 這會削弱長債作為股票風險對沖的直覺判斷，尤其在實質利率上行時，兩者可能同時承壓；因此風險代表應看 SOX/NDX 的折現率衝擊，而不是只看 S&P 500。

AI 融資與 IPO 供給把股市訊號延伸到信用市場。Apollo/Anthropic 350 億美元晶片融資、SpaceX 750 億美元 IPO 需求，以及大型資料中心與電力投資，都顯示 AI 題材需要持續吸收外部資本。當 5Y-10Y 殖利率重新上行，AI 不只是股票長久期題材，也會影響私募信用、IG 替代供給與長端資金需求。 這些資金需求未必立即推高 UST 殖利率，但會提高長端資本的競爭度，並讓 IG 與私募信用在高利率環境下面對更挑剔的定價。

LQD 是這次信用代理的重要觀察。LQD 收 108.17、日跌 0.62%，HYG 收 79.43、日跌 0.50%，代表 IG Duration 與 HY 風險曝險都承壓。IG 本身仍有收益吸引力，但當 LQD 與 UST 同跌時，信用市場不能只用穩定或不穩定概括；真正要拆的是 Duration 損失、利差補償、發行品質與 ETF 流動性。 如果後續 LQD 穩住而 UST 繼續弱，問題偏 Duration；若 LQD 與 HYG 同跌，信用利差與流動性壓力就需要更高權重。

信用事件補充的是尾端風險分布。Raizen 債務重組、AI 相關融資、以及日本企業槓桿上升，都反映資金成本上升後的微觀壓力。這些事件不代表系統性信用風險已經發生，但會讓低品質或長天期信用在利率上行時缺乏保護。美元債 ETF 可保留 IG 收益來源，HY 與弱主權則需要更高風險補償。 對 ETF 分類而言，高品質 IG 可以保留為收益來源，但低品質信用與 EM 主權不能只因票息高就提高權重；資產負債表與再融資能力更重要。

油價與地緣風險同時影響通膨與成長。周五 Brent 回落至 93.09，但亞洲盤反彈到約 95.60，代表能源風險仍會干擾 CPI 與 Fed 語句。對債券 ETF 而言，油價不是單純商品變數；Brent 92 下方會改善通膨溢價，Brent 98 上方則會提高實質所得壓力與長端損益平衡通膨。 若油價下行與 CPI 降溫同時出現，Duration 的勝率會改善；若油價反彈與就業強勢並存，市場會把高殖利率視為風險補償而非便宜。

Indonesia 與日本訊號屬於亞洲盤傳導風險。印尼股指距高點約 -39%、印尼盾年內約 -8% 並曾破 18,000，說明 EM 本地債需要更高實質殖利率與更可信政策組合；日本科技股與 JGB 波動則影響全球長端風險承接。這些訊號不主導 06/05 UST 收盤，但會影響隔日亞洲信用、FX 與主權利差。 因此 Asia/EM 訊號適合作為隔日風險承接的檢查點：若美元與美債同時施壓，弱 FX 國家的信用與本地債會先反映流動性折價。

美元與台幣必須用產品角度拆解。CBC USD/TWD 收 31.475，較前一交易日升 0.015，對台幣計價美元債 ETF 的 NAV 換算是正向，因為底層資產是美元債券但基金以台幣計價。不過同一天 10Y 上升 8bp、LQD 下跌 0.62%，匯兌助益可能被債券美元價格損失抵銷，總報酬仍需拆成 FX、UST 與信用三條線。 這個拆分能避免把台幣走弱誤讀成無條件利多：FX 換算支撐 NAV，但若 UST 與 IG 價格同時下跌，最終台幣總報酬仍可能偏弱。

sections.title: 四、配置含義
sections.takeaway: 配置以中段 Duration、IG 收益與分段歸因為主，長端等待確認。
paragraphs: Duration 配置維持中性偏多，但主要觀察區應放在 5Y-10Y。10Y 4.55% 已比前一周高，提供較好的進場殖利率；可是非農驚喜、CPI swap 4.3% 與 FOMC 語句風險都表示現在不適合一次拉滿部位。若用台灣上市美債 ETF 表達，必須確認商品實際 Duration 與追蹤指數，避免用看似中天期的收益率承擔接近長天期的價格波動。 若 CPI 前要建立部位，較穩健的方式是小額分批並保留現金彈性，讓資料公布後仍有空間調整，而不是在單日高殖利率出現後一次買滿。

長天期美債 ETF 仍不適合追價。30Y 5.01% 看似提供高收益，但它沒有在股市大跌時明顯修復，代表長端買盤尚未完全回來。若 30Y 站上 5.05%，20Y+ ETF 的價格風險會再度放大；若 30Y 回到 4.95% 下方並且 Brent 低於 92，期限溢價和通膨溢價才比較像同時降溫。 對 20Y+ ETF，這表示高殖利率只是補償，不是風險解除；若投資人以長端商品追求配息，仍需承擔更大的利率 convexity 與 NAV drawdown。

Curve 配置要避免追逐已經走了一段的壓平。2s10s 38bp、5s30s 72bp 已反映相當多前端重定價，繼續追壓平需要 CPI 或 FOMC 語句再給確認。比較好的做法是中段超配、長端小幅低配，保留 income 與 roll-down，同時降低 30Y 受供給與期限溢價重新上行的傷害。 這個配置也讓 ETF 組合在兩種情境下都有彈性：若 CPI 降溫，中段先修復；若 CPI 偏高，長端低配可降低 30Y 再上行的損失。

信用配置偏向 IG 收益與短中天期，不宜用 HY 追風險。LQD -0.62% 顯示 IG 也受 Duration 影響，但 IG 的票息與品質仍優於低品質信用；HYG -0.50% 則提醒 HY 並不是避險資產。對台灣美元債 ETF 投資人而言，LQD 是 IG 總報酬代理指標，後續更重要的是利差補償、一級市場品質、ETF 流動性與折溢價，而不是單日漲跌。 若沒有直接利差資料，LQD 與 HYG 只能作為總報酬代理指標；正式配置仍要看指數 Duration、信用評級、產業集中度與 ETF 流動性。

台幣計價海外債 ETF 要把 FX 換算、UST 價格與信用利差拆開。USD/TWD 31.475 表示匯率換算助益增加；因底層持有美元債，台幣走弱時，換算後的台幣 NAV 通常得到支撐。同一天仍要另外扣除 UST 價格與 LQD 表現，因為債券美元價格下跌可能吃掉一部分匯兌利益。 這種分解對台灣產品特別重要，因為同一天可能出現 FX 支撐、利率拖累與信用拖累並存；只看 NAV 單日變化容易錯判真正來源。

高配息或長天期 ETF 仍要用總報酬衡量。殖利率提高確實改善未來收益，但短期價格損失會先反映在 NAV；若利率上行速度快於收益累積，高配息也無法立即保護本金。比較務實的評估方式是把配息率、Duration、指數殖利率、匯率換算與信用曝險同時列出，不把單一高配息當作防守訊號。 對零售與 PM 都一樣，配息率只是現金流呈現方式，總報酬仍取決於價格、匯率、利差與費用；高配息不能取代風險預算。ETF 歸因只需在這裡總結一次：FX translation、UST price return 與 credit spread return 要分開看。

實作上，5Y-10Y 可用小部位等待 CPI 驗證，20Y+ 則等 30Y 回到 4.95% 下方或 Brent 跌破 92 再提高信心。信用部位以 IG 與短中天期為核心，HY 與弱主權只在利差補償明顯擴大時才重新評估。若 CPI 接近 4.3%、10Y 上破 4.65%、30Y 站上 5.05%、LQD 跌破 108，配置重心應轉回流動性與高品質收益。 這組條件讓配置語氣保持中立而具操作性：有殖利率可以準備，但還沒有足夠證據把中段觀察升級成長端全面加碼。

sections.title: 五、後續觀察重點
sections.takeaway: 觀察順序為 CPI/FOMC、10Y/30Y、Brent、LQD/HYG、USD/TWD 與 Asia transmission。
paragraphs: 第一個觸發是 CPI。若 6 月 10 日 CPI 接近 swap 隱含的 4.3%，前端會重新測試 Fed 保留升息選項，10Y 可能往 4.65% 靠近，5Y-10Y 部位維持觀望；若 CPI 低於 4.1%，非農衝擊缺乏通膨確認，中段 Duration 才有更好的修復條件。 對 ETF 操作而言，這是一個先後順序明確的資料點：CPI 先決定前端路徑，才輪到 10Y 是否突破區間與長端是否能修復；低於 4.1% 是 add 條件，接近 4.3% 是等待條件。

第二個觸發是 FOMC 語句。6 月 16-17 日聲明若移除偏降息語氣，或重新強調通膨韌性，2Y 與 5Y 對政策尾端風險會更敏感；若聲明保留雙向彈性且承認就業之外的成長放緩，市場才有機會把升息定價降回風險情境。這會直接影響短中天期 ETF 的再配置節奏。 若 2Y-5Y 對聲明反應過大，短中天期 ETF 可能先承壓；若反應溫和而 10Y 回落，才代表政策風險被重新壓回可承受範圍。這裡的角色是政策確認，不是新的獨立配置結論。

第三個觸發是 10Y 與 30Y 價位。10Y 下破 4.45% 才代表中段買盤真正接手，4.55%-4.65% 則是風險管理區；30Y 若站上 5.05%，代表期限溢價和供給壓力重新主導，20Y+ ETF 需降低價格彈性假設。若 10Y 回落但 30Y 仍高於 5%，修復主要集中在中段。 這兩個價位要一起看，因為 10Y 回落只能說明中段買盤改善，30Y 回落才代表長端期限溢價也被壓住；兩者缺一時不宜追長端。
第四個觸發是油價。Brent 92 下方有助通膨溢價降溫，也能讓 Fed 語句比較容易保持彈性；Brent 98 上方則會重新推升能源通膨、實質所得壓力與長端損益平衡通膨。對 ETF 配置而言，油價走低會讓高殖利率更像機會，油價上衝則讓高殖利率更像風險補償。 油價的角色應固定為長端修復與 Fed 語氣的外生約束：92 下方增加加碼勝率，98 上方把高殖利率重新定義成風險補償。

第五個觸發是信用代理。LQD 108 是 IG 總報酬代理指標 的近端支撐，HYG 79 是 HY 景氣曝險 的風險線；若 LQD 跌破 108 且 HYG 跌破 79，代表利率與信用同時轉弱，不宜用高配息或 FX 換算掩蓋總報酬壓力。若 LQD 穩住、HYG 不再破底，IG 收益部位才比較能保留。 這組信用觸發可以把 IG 與 HY 分開處理：IG 穩定代表收益仍可留存，HY 失守則代表市場已要求景氣與流動性折價。

第六個觸發是 USD/TWD。31.40 下方代表台幣走強、FX 換算助益減弱，台幣計價海外債 ETF 會更依賴 UST 與信用表現；31.55 上方則表示匯率換算助益增加。判讀必須使用台灣央行銀行間收盤或台北外匯經紀公司境內收盤匯率，離岸報價只能作為參考。 若境內收盤與離岸報價分歧，ETF 歸因仍以境內收盤為主，因為台幣計價產品的 NAV 換算最接近本地收盤匯率。USD/TWD 是歸因觀察點，不是單獨加碼觸發。

最後觀察亞洲與 AI 傳導。Indonesia 若能讓 USD/IDR 回到 18,000 下方並提出可信政策組合，EM 風險可穩定；SOX 若回到 12,800 上方且 10Y 同時低於 4.50%，折現率壓力才算緩和。若 SOX 失守 12,000 且 LQD 續跌，AI 會同時牽動資金需求、信用供給與長端資金吸收。 這些檢查不改變美國利率主軸，但能判斷壓力是否從 UST 擴散到亞洲信用、科技融資與 EM 主權風險。

en.title: Daily FI Market Tape | 2026-06-05 close
summary: Payrolls +172k beat roughly +80k consensus, lifting the 2Y to 4.17% and the 5Y to 4.29% as the Fed hike-option risk moved back into the front end and belly.
The 10Y closed at 4.55% and the 30Y at 5.01%; 2s10s and 5s30s both flattened, showing long-end buying is still constrained by term premium and supply.
SOX -10.3%, NDX -4.8% and LQD -0.6% point to higher discount rates and funding costs, not a clean risk-off Treasury bid.
CBC USD/TWD closed at 31.475. TWD overseas-bond ETFs get FX translation support, but higher rates and weaker credit proxies can offset part of the NAV benefit.

headline.primary: Strong payrolls lift front-end yields; bear flattening keeps long-duration ETFs under pressure
headline.secondary: 2Y 4.17%, 10Y 4.55%, 2s10s 38bp; watch the belly first and wait for CPI, the 30Y and oil before extending into 20Y+.

regime_strip.label: Rates
regime_strip.value: Front end and belly reprice the Fed path

regime_strip.label: Risk
regime_strip.value: SOX / NDX confirm a discount-rate shock

regime_strip.label: Credit
regime_strip.value: LQD 108 is the near IG check

positioning.label: Duration / 5Y-10Y UST
positioning.best_expression: 10Y at 4.55%-4.60% is a watch zone; small staging is different from a full add.
positioning.risk_trigger: Add: CPI < 4.1%, Brent < 92 and 10Y back to 4.45%-4.50%; stop: 10Y > 4.65.

positioning.label: Curve / 20Y+ UST
positioning.best_expression: Do not chase the long end until the 30Y is below 4.95%; above 5% is still risk compensation.
positioning.risk_trigger: Add only if 30Y < 4.95 and Brent < 92; stop if 30Y > 5.05.

positioning.label: Credit / IG
positioning.best_expression: Keep IG income and quality while LQD is above 108; do not use HY beta for cyclical risk.
positioning.risk_trigger: LQD below 108 means IG total-return pressure is widening, so slow credit exposure.

positioning.label: Credit / HY beta
positioning.best_expression: Do not chase cyclical beta through high coupon until HYG is above 79 and SOX stabilizes.
positioning.risk_trigger: If HYG < 79 or LQD/HYG fall together, return first to quality and liquidity.

positioning.label: FX / NAV
positioning.best_expression: USD/TWD 31.40 / 31.55 is for TWD NAV translation attribution, not a standalone add signal.
positioning.risk_trigger: FX support must be checked against UST prices and LQD; TWD weakness is not automatically positive total return.

driver_decomposition.fi_impact: This is the first-order June 5 driver: markets repriced the Fed keeping a hike option alive, with 2Y-5Y most directly pressured.

driver_decomposition.signal: CPI is still pending; swaps imply roughly 4.3% y/y.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: CPI is a forward trigger, not a completed driver. Below 4.1% is needed to move 5Y-10Y from watch toward add.
Long-end buying has not taken control; 20Y+ ETF convexity remains constrained by term premium, supply and incomplete haven demand.

driver_decomposition.signal: SOX -10.3% and Nasdaq 100 -4.8%, worse than S&P 500 and Dow.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: Cross-asset signals point to higher discount rates compressing long-duration cash flows, not a traditional risk-off move that creates a long-end bid.

driver_decomposition.signal: Anthropic chip financing and SpaceX IPO demand show large capital absorption is still active.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: AI funding is not the first-order UST close driver. It is a structural overlay for long-end capital demand, IG substitutes and term-premium stickiness.

driver_decomposition.signal: Brent closed Friday at 93.09 and rebounded toward 95.60 in Asia.
driver_decomposition.fi_impact: Oil is an external constraint on long-end repair and Fed language: below 92 cools the premium, above 98 raises long-end risk compensation.
Dollar strength supports TWD NAV translation, but the formal read still has to split FX translation, UST price return and credit spread return.

key_levels.label: 10Y UST
key_levels.detail: Below 4.45% shows belly buyers are back; above 4.65% pauses duration adds.

key_levels.label: 30Y UST
key_levels.detail: A clear break higher means term premium and supply pressure again dominate 20Y+ ETF risk.

key_levels.label: LQD
key_levels.detail: A break below 108 means IG credit and rates losses are widening together.

key_levels.label: USD/TWD
key_levels.detail: Below 31.40 weakens FX support; above 31.55 still requires separating FX gains from bond-price losses.

event_risks.label: U.S. CPI
event_risks.detail: Near 4.3% reinforces the Fed hike-option risk; below 4.1% supports belly repair.

event_risks.label: FOMC
event_risks.detail: Removing cut-friendly language would keep 2Y-5Y more sensitive than the long end.

event_risks.label: Oil
event_risks.detail: Below 92 cools inflation premium; above 98 revives long-end pressure.

event_risks.label: Credit
event_risks.detail: Both breaking lower would signal rates and credit stress together; do not rely only on FX translation.

asia_update.label: Taiwan FX
asia_update.note: TWD overseas-bond ETFs first need FX translation, UST price return and credit spread return separated.

asia_update.label: Japan
asia_update.note: Japan tech and JGB volatility affect Asia long-end risk absorption, but do not drive the June 5 UST close.

asia_update.label: Indonesia
asia_update.value: EM FX defense remains the focus
asia_update.note: Indonesia policy risk is useful for EM/Asia transmission and should not sit ahead of the U.S. Fed/labor line.

rows.label: SOX
rows.note: Chip shares are the clearest discount-rate shock signal and affect Asia tech plus AI funding risk absorption.

rows.label: Nasdaq 100
rows.note: The sharpest drop since April 2025 shows higher discount rates compressed high-growth cash flows.

rows.label: Nasdaq Composite
rows.note: AI and long-duration growth equities were most sensitive to front-end repricing, falling more than value stocks.

rows.label: DXY
rows.note: Dollar-rate advantage rose again, confirming real-yield and policy-rate repricing is pressuring Asia FX.

rows.label: Gold
rows.note: Higher real yields and the stronger dollar outweighed haven demand.

rows.label: LQD / IG Credit
rows.note: IG credit and duration weakened together; focus on spread compensation, primary quality and ETF liquidity.

rows.label: HYG / HY Credit
rows.note: HY is not a safe asset; if it falls with LQD, rates and credit pressure are rising together.

rows.label: S&P 500
rows.note: Broad equity weakness is context, but it is not the sharpest representative of today’s discount-rate shock.

rows.label: Dow
rows.note: Defensive and value shares held up better but could not offset the rates shock.

rows.label: USD/TWD
rows.note: The Taipei close showed a small USD gain; TWD overseas-bond ETFs get FX translation support, but rates and credit need separate attribution.

rows.label: Brent
rows.note: Friday’s close was lower, but Asia trading rebounded toward $95.60, so energy inflation premium has not cleared.

rows.label: U.S. payrolls
rows.expected: +80k consensus; unemployment 4.3%; April revised to +179k
rows.note: The labor beat is the first-order Fed-path driver for this tape, with 2Y-5Y most exposed.

rows.label: Fed pricing
rows.value: 25bp year-end hike fully priced; October about 60%
rows.expected: June meeting expected to stay on hold
rows.note: Once hike-tail risk is priced, the front end is more sensitive than the long end to FOMC language.

rows.label: U.S. CPI
rows.expected: Near the highest since 2023; prior was below the implied level
rows.note: CPI is the forward trigger. Near 4.3% extends front-end pressure; below 4.1% raises confidence in belly adds.

rows.label: Treasury supply / Term premium
rows.expected: Long end must absorb supply, issuance and hedging pressure
rows.note: 30Y above 5.05% would show term premium and funding pressure overpowering haven demand.

rows.label: Oil / inflation premium
rows.expected: 92 / 98 trigger
rows.note: Oil is an external constraint on long-end repair and Fed language: below 92 cools the premium, above 98 adds pressure.

rows.label: AI / structural capital absorption
rows.value: Anthropic chip financing $35bn; SpaceX IPO target $75bn
rows.expected: AI capex and large IPO supply keep absorbing capital
rows.note: AI funding is not the day’s first-order UST driver; it is a structural overlay for long-end capital demand and IG substitutes.

rows.label: LQD / HYG credit proxy
rows.expected: LQD 108 / HYG 79
rows.note: Use this to split duration loss from spread pressure; reduce credit beta if both break lower.

rows.label: Japan policy
rows.value: Nikkei futures 63,865 vs spot 66,588; BOJ hike expectations rose
rows.expected: BOJ meeting next week
rows.note: Japan / JGB is an Asia transmission check, not the U.S. labor/Fed core.

rows.label: Indonesia policy
rows.value: Equities about -39% from high; rupiah about -8% YTD and breached 18,000
rows.expected: BI 6/18; market debates whether more than 50bp, possibly 75bp, is needed
rows.note: EM local debt needs higher real yields and credible policy; use it as Asia transmission context.

rows.label: U.S. wages
rows.expected: Near prior; market focus is whether CPI again exceeds wage growth
rows.note: Wages were not disorderly, but did not neutralize the payroll surprise; CPI is the next confirmation point.

rows.label: Canada jobs
rows.expected: +10k consensus; unemployment 6.9%
rows.note: Canada confirms broader North American resilience but remains secondary to U.S. CPI/FOMC.

sections.title: 1. Treasury Close and Curve Move
sections.takeaway: Strong payrolls lifted Treasury front-end yields and flattened the curve while long-end buying stayed constrained.
paragraphs: The June 5 Treasury close was led by a stronger labor print that pushed the Fed path back toward a higher-rate distribution. Official CMT levels put the 2Y at 4.17%, up 12bp on the day, the 5Y at 4.29%, up 11bp, and the 10Y at 4.55%, up 8bp, while the 20Y and 30Y closed at 5.03% and 5.01%. The move was front-end and belly-led, so the market first repriced policy-rate risk and then demanded more compensation further out the curve. For short/intermediate ETF exposure, that means better entry yield comes with faster price transmission; without inflation relief, carry alone is not enough to offset another move higher in front-end rates.

The curve message was bear flattening. 2s10s closed at 38bp, 4bp flatter on the day; 5s30s closed at 72bp, 7bp flatter; and 10s30s closed at 46bp, 4bp flatter. That shape says 2Y-5Y reacted most directly to Fed pricing, while the 30Y stayed constrained by term premium, supply absorption and oil-linked inflation risk. For ETFs, the belly has the cleaner source of volatility; 20Y+ price convexity is still less clean. It also widens attribution differences across ETF tenors: 5Y-10Y is mainly about Fed and CPI pricing, while 20Y+ also carries supply, term premium and weak long-end demand.

The one-week and one-month changes show this was not one-day noise. The 2Y is up 19bp on the week and 24bp on the month; the 5Y is up 16bp and 21bp; the 10Y is up 10bp and 12bp. 2s10s has flattened 12bp over the month and 5s30s has flattened 18bp. The market has moved from asking when cuts arrive to checking whether the Fed needs to keep a hike option alive. If CPI confirms sticky inflation, the front end and belly can still cheapen further. For positioning, the weekly and monthly changes matter more than the cash close because they show a sustained repricing of the policy path; without data confirmation, short rallies can be only position adjustment.

The most important cross-check is that the long end did not rally cleanly despite the equity selloff. The 30Y stayed at 5.01% and the 20Y at 5.03%, even with Nasdaq 100 and SOX down sharply. Long-duration Treasury ETFs therefore cannot rely on equity weakness alone for NAV repair. Price recovery requires CPI not to accelerate, oil to settle lower, supply pressure to ease and the 30Y to move back below 5%. Long-end ETF success therefore depends not just on equity risk tone but on whether long-end investors will accept a 30Y below 5%; that condition has not been confirmed yet.

The data gave the front end a direct reason to reprice. Payrolls were +172k versus roughly +80k consensus, unemployment held at 4.3%, and April payrolls were revised up to +179k. Average hourly earnings at 3.4% y/y were not runaway, but they did not neutralize the jobs surprise. The package made it difficult to keep pricing a rapid labor-market deterioration, which is why the 2Y and 5Y carried the largest pressure. For short/intermediate ETFs, labor data remain the first-order sensitivity; if jobless claims or wages do not soften alongside payrolls, front-end rally durability is limited. For short/intermediate ETFs, labor remains the first-order sensitivity. If jobless claims or wages do not soften alongside payrolls, front-end rally durability is limited. This supports a watch zone, not a full 5Y-10Y add yet.

Oil is the second constraint on long-end repair. Brent closed Friday at 93.09, down 2.0%, and WTI at 90.54, down 2.7%, which looked helpful for inflation premium at the cash close. But Brent rebounded toward 95.60 in Asia, showing that geopolitics and energy risk have not fully left the Fed reaction function. If Brent returns above 98, breakeven inflation and real-income pressure rise again, and duration exposure needs tighter stops and staging. This channel feeds directly into 10Y and 30Y risk compensation and into principal volatility for high-distribution bond ETFs, because energy pressure first hits yields rather than distribution rates.

The first read for Taiwan bond ETFs is therefore higher short/intermediate rate risk, a long end still constrained by term premium, and simultaneous pressure on credit and equity-duration assets. The 10Y at 4.55% offers better carry, but it still needs CPI and FOMC language confirmation. The 30Y at 5.01% says near-term price risk in 20Y+ ETFs remains larger than the simple appeal of higher yield. The appropriate stance for the day is preparation and staging, not treating higher yield as a blanket add signal; the confirmation points remain CPI, the FOMC, the 30Y and Brent.

sections.title: 2. Rates Interpretation
sections.takeaway: CPI and FOMC language decide whether 10Y 4.55% is an entry zone or an interim pressure point.
paragraphs: The core rates change is that the Fed reaction function has moved from waiting for cut conditions to re-establishing inflation credibility. After the jobs release, the market priced a 25bp hike by year-end and took October hike probability toward roughly 60%. That does not mean the Fed must hike, but it means the front end can no longer price cuts as a one-way path. Investors holding 5Y-10Y or longer ETFs need to weigh higher yield against price volatility. In this setup, ETF investors first need to choose which part of the curve to underwrite, not simply compare headline yields; an unstable front-end path amplifies NAV volatility in long-duration funds.

CPI is the next divider. Swaps imply roughly 4.3% y/y for this week. If the print is near that level, 10Y at 4.55% may be an interim pressure point and the market can retest 4.65%, while the June 16-17 FOMC statement would need to remove cut-friendly language. If CPI is below 4.1%, the payroll shock lacks inflation confirmation and the front end and belly have a better chance to repair. That is why 5Y-10Y is the better first observation zone than 20Y+: the belly can respond faster to policy-expectation repair, while the long end still needs term premium and supply signals to improve.

Policy communication also matters more now. Goldman has removed its expected Fed cut for this year, while Citi remains on a more dovish path, so forecast dispersion itself raises sensitivity to official wording. Whether the 2Y holds 4.15% and the 5Y holds 4.30% will say more about policy-end risk than a single equity move. Jobs data and Fed pricing should be treated as one policy mechanism. If those levels stay high, the market is still demanding compensation for policy uncertainty; if they fall, investors are again accepting the cut path and belly duration.

For curve trades, chasing front-end shorts and buying the long end are both imperfect. 2s10s is already at 38bp and 5s30s at 72bp, so flatteners have already moved. At the same time, the 30Y remains at 5.01%, and sticky supply or term premium would make early 20Y+ buying a different risk. The cleaner observation zone is 5Y-10Y; confidence in the long end rises only if the 30Y breaks back below 4.95% and oil eases. For ETF implementation, curve slope is not just a trade label; it decides whether the belly or the long end is the better place to take risk. Without 30Y confirmation, long-end weight should not rise quickly.

Long-end pressure is not one event; several risk premia are present at once. Fiscal supply, long-dated issuance absorption, MBS hedging and oil-linked inflation premium all influence whether the 30Y can stabilize below 5%. AI financing and large IPO supply should not be treated as the same-level cause of the June 5 UST selloff. They belong in a structural capital-absorption check: do long-end capital demand and IG substitutes make it harder for the 30Y to move back below 5%? Taiwan investors holding 20Y+ ETFs experience this risk directly through TWD NAV volatility; high-distribution products still need income separated from principal risk.

Canada jobs at +87.8k and unemployment at 6.6% are secondary evidence. They are not the core UST driver, but they show North American demand resilience is not just a U.S. story. For Taiwan investors, that matters through the discount rate on dollar bond ETFs and through whether central-bank narratives can quickly return to cuts. This secondary evidence can increase confidence that front-end rates are sticky, but it should not outrank U.S. CPI and the FOMC. It mainly lowers the odds of adding too early.

The stronger dollar and weaker gold also fit a higher real-rate interpretation. DXY rose 0.66% and gold fell 3.1%, so the market was repricing dollar-rate advantage and real yield. For TWD-denominated overseas-bond ETFs, a stronger dollar helps TWD NAV translation, but higher real rates pressure UST and IG bond prices in dollars. Those return channels must be separated before drawing a total-return conclusion. FX contribution to NAV is only one attribution line and cannot cover negative rates and credit price returns, especially for unhedged or lightly hedged products.

sections.title: 3. Cross-Market Read
sections.takeaway: Cross-asset pressure points to higher discount rates and funding costs; Asia and EM are transmission checks.
paragraphs: The risk-asset message is concentrated in long-duration cash flows being compressed by higher discount rates. Nasdaq 100 fell 4.8% and SOX fell 10.3%, far more than the Dow at -1.3%, showing that the market sold the AI and semiconductor cash flows most sensitive to rates. The FI implication is that growth equities and long-duration bonds can face the same discount-rate pressure rather than acting as automatic hedges. That weakens the intuitive use of long bonds as an equity hedge, especially when real rates are rising. The risk representative should therefore be SOX/NDX as the discount-rate shock, not S&P 500 alone.

AI financing and IPO supply extend that equity signal into credit. Apollo/Anthropic chip financing of $35bn, SpaceX IPO demand around $75bn, and large data-center and power investment all point to sustained external capital absorption. When 5Y-10Y yields rise again, AI is not only an equity-duration theme; it also affects private credit, IG substitute supply and demand for long-end capital. These funding needs may not immediately lift UST yields, but they raise competition for long-end capital and make IG and private credit pricing more selective in a high-rate environment.

LQD is the important credit proxy for this tape. LQD closed at 108.17, down 0.62%, while HYG closed at 79.43, down 0.50%, so IG duration and HY beta were both under pressure. IG still has income appeal, but when LQD and UST weaken together, credit cannot be summarized as stable or unstable. The useful split is duration loss, spread compensation, primary quality and ETF liquidity. If LQD stabilizes while USTs keep weakening, the issue is mostly duration; if LQD and HYG both fall, spread and liquidity stress need more weight.

Credit events add information about tail distribution. Raizen restructuring, AI-related financing and rising Japanese corporate leverage all reflect micro pressure from higher funding costs. They do not mean systemic credit stress has arrived, but they reduce protection for lower-quality or long-duration credit when rates rise. Dollar bond ETFs can retain IG income exposure, while HY and weak sovereign risk need more compensation. For ETF classification, high-quality IG can remain an income source, but low-quality credit and EM sovereigns should not get more weight just because coupons are high; balance-sheet and refinancing capacity matter more.

Oil and geopolitics affect both inflation and growth. Brent fell to 93.09 at Friday’s close but rebounded toward 95.60 in Asia, so energy risk can still interfere with CPI and Fed language. For bond ETFs, oil is not just a commodity variable. Brent below 92 improves inflation premium, while Brent above 98 raises real-income pressure and long-end breakeven inflation. If lower oil and softer CPI arrive together, duration odds improve; if oil rebounds while labor stays strong, the market will treat high yield as risk compensation rather than cheap carry.

Indonesia and Japan are Asia-session transmission risks. Indonesian equities are roughly 39% below their high, and the rupiah is down about 8% year to date after breaking 18,000; EM local bonds need higher real yields and more credible policy. Japan tech pressure and JGB volatility affect global long-end risk absorption. These signals do not drive the June 5 UST close, but they matter for next-session Asia credit, FX and sovereign spreads. Asia and EM signals are therefore next-session transmission checks: when the dollar and Treasuries pressure markets together, weak-FX countries first show liquidity discounts in credit and local bonds.

The dollar and Taiwan dollar need product-level attribution. CBC USD/TWD closed at 31.475, up 0.015 from the prior trading day, which is positive for TWD NAV translation because the underlying assets are dollar bonds reported in TWD. On the same day, however, the 10Y rose 8bp and LQD fell 0.62%, so FX gains can be offset by dollar bond price losses. Total return must be split into FX, UST and credit legs. This split avoids reading TWD weakness as an unconditional positive: FX translation supports NAV, but final TWD total return can still be weak if UST and IG prices fall together.

sections.title: 4. Allocation Read
sections.takeaway: Keep the bias in belly duration, IG income and segmented attribution; wait for confirmation before adding long-end risk.
paragraphs: Duration remains neutral to slightly long, but the main observation zone is 5Y-10Y. The 10Y at 4.55% is cheaper than last week and offers a better entry yield, but the payroll beat, CPI swaps near 4.3% and FOMC language risk argue against filling the position all at once. For Taiwan-listed Treasury ETFs, verify the actual duration and index exposure so a seemingly intermediate yield does not carry near long-end price volatility. If exposure is built before CPI, the steadier method is small staging with cash flexibility so the portfolio can still adjust after the data instead of filling the whole position on one high-yield day.

Long-duration Treasury ETFs should not be chased. The 30Y at 5.01% offers high yield, but it did not repair materially during the equity selloff, so long-end buyers have not fully returned. If the 30Y breaks 5.05%, 20Y+ ETF price risk rises again. If the 30Y falls below 4.95% while Brent is below 92, term premium and inflation premium would look more clearly improved together. For 20Y+ ETFs, high yield is compensation, not proof that risk has cleared; investors using long-end products for distribution still carry larger rate convexity and NAV drawdown.

Curve positioning should avoid chasing a move that has already travelled. 2s10s at 38bp and 5s30s at 72bp already reflect significant front-end repricing. More flattening needs confirmation from CPI or the FOMC statement. A better expression is belly overweight and small long-end underweight: keep income and roll-down while reducing exposure to another term-premium or supply-driven selloff in the 30Y. This allocation also leaves room in both scenarios: if CPI cools, the belly repairs first; if CPI is hot, the long-end underweight reduces losses from another 30Y move higher.

Credit exposure should favor IG income and short/intermediate maturity rather than HY risk. LQD -0.62% shows IG is still affected by duration, but IG coupon and quality remain better than low-quality credit. HYG -0.50% reminds that HY is not a safe asset. For Taiwan dollar-bond ETF investors, LQD is an IG total-return proxy; the follow-up checks are spread compensation, primary-market quality, ETF liquidity and premium/discount, not the single-day price move alone. Without direct spread data, LQD and HYG are only total-return proxies; formal allocation still needs index duration, ratings mix, sector concentration and ETF liquidity.

TWD-denominated overseas-bond ETFs need FX translation, UST price return and credit spread return separated. USD/TWD at 31.475 means FX translation support increased. Because the funds hold dollar bonds but report in TWD, TWD depreciation usually supports translated NAV. The same day still requires deducting UST and LQD price performance because dollar bond losses can absorb part of the FX benefit. This decomposition matters especially for Taiwan products because FX support, rates drag and credit drag can coexist on the same day; looking only at one-day NAV can misidentify the real driver.

High-distribution or long-duration ETFs still need total-return discipline. Higher yields improve future income, but near-term price losses show up in NAV first. If yields rise faster than income accrues, distribution yield does not immediately protect principal. A practical review should list distribution rate, duration, index yield, FX translation and credit exposure together rather than treating high distribution as a defensive signal. For retail investors and PMs alike, distribution rate is only a cash-flow presentation. Total return still comes from price, FX, spreads and fees. The ETF attribution summary belongs here once: split FX translation, UST price return and credit spread return.

Implementation can stay staged. A small 5Y-10Y position can wait for CPI validation, while 20Y+ should wait for the 30Y below 4.95% or Brent below 92 before confidence rises. Credit should center on IG and short/intermediate maturity; HY and weaker sovereign risk only deserve reconsideration when spread compensation has clearly widened. If CPI is near 4.3%, 10Y breaks 4.65%, 30Y breaks 5.05% or LQD loses 108, the allocation center should return to liquidity and quality income. The condition set keeps the stance neutral but actionable: yield is high enough to prepare, but evidence is not strong enough to upgrade belly observation into broad long-end adding.

sections.title: 5. Watchlist
sections.takeaway: Watch CPI/FOMC, 10Y/30Y, Brent, LQD/HYG, USD/TWD and Asia transmission in that order.
paragraphs: The first trigger is CPI. If the June 10 print is near the roughly 4.3% swap-implied level, the front end will retest the Fed hike-option risk, the 10Y can move toward 4.65%, and 5Y-10Y positions should stay patient. If CPI is below 4.1%, the payroll shock lacks inflation confirmation and belly duration has a better repair setup. For ETF execution, this is a clear sequencing point: CPI first sets the front-end path, then the 10Y range and the long-end repair test matter. Below 4.1% is an add condition; near 4.3% is a wait condition.

The second trigger is FOMC language. If the June 16-17 statement removes cut-friendly language or stresses inflation resilience again, the 2Y and 5Y remain more sensitive to policy-end risk than the long end. If the statement keeps two-way flexibility and acknowledges growth cooling beyond employment, the market can mark the hike pricing back into a risk scenario. If 2Y-5Y react too sharply to the statement, short/intermediate ETFs can weaken first; if the reaction is mild and the 10Y falls, policy risk is moving back into an acceptable range. This is policy confirmation, not a separate allocation conclusion.

The third trigger is the 10Y and 30Y. A 10Y break below 4.45% would show that belly buyers are taking control, while 4.55%-4.65% remains the risk-management zone. A 30Y break above 5.05% would signal that term premium and supply pressure dominate again, so 20Y+ ETF price-convexity assumptions should be reduced. If the 10Y rallies but the 30Y stays above 5%, the repair is mostly in the belly. The two levels must be read together: a lower 10Y only says belly demand improved, while a lower 30Y says long-end term premium is also contained. Without both, do not chase the long end.

The fourth trigger is oil. Brent below 92 helps inflation premium cool and gives the Fed more room to keep language flexible. Brent above 98 would lift energy inflation, real-income pressure and long-end breakeven inflation. For ETF allocation, lower oil makes high yield look more like opportunity; higher oil makes high yield look more like required risk compensation. Oil should keep one role: the external constraint on long-end repair and Fed language. Below 92 raises add confidence; above 98 turns high yield back into required risk compensation.

The fifth trigger is credit proxies. LQD 108 is the near support level for the IG total-return proxy, and HYG 79 is the HY beta risk line. If both break lower, rates and credit are weakening together, so high distribution or FX translation should not hide total-return pressure. If LQD stabilizes and HYG stops making new lows, IG income exposure can be retained more comfortably. These credit triggers separate IG from HY: stable IG means income can be retained, while weaker HY means markets are demanding cyclical and liquidity discounts.

The sixth trigger is USD/TWD. Below 31.40 means TWD strength and weaker FX translation support, leaving TWD overseas-bond ETFs more dependent on UST and credit performance. Above 31.55 means FX translation support is stronger. The reference must be the Taiwan central-bank interbank close or Taipei Forex Inc onshore close; offshore quotes should only be treated as reference. If the onshore close and offshore quote diverge, ETF attribution should still use the onshore close because TWD product NAV translation is closest to the local closing rate. USD/TWD is an attribution trigger, not a standalone add trigger.

The last checks are Asia and AI transmission. If Indonesia can pull USD/IDR back below 18,000 with a credible policy mix, EM risk can stabilize. If SOX returns above 12,800 while the 10Y is below 4.50%, discount-rate pressure has eased. If SOX loses 12,000 and LQD keeps falling, AI is simultaneously affecting funding demand, credit supply and long-end capital absorption. These checks do not change the U.S. rates core, but they show whether pressure is spreading from USTs into Asia credit, tech financing and EM sovereign risk.
